scorecards.name: Ezra
scorecards.role: Daily Brief Editor — edits and ships Marc's 5:45am morning brief; synthesizes Daniel's research digest, Martha's calendar brief, and Lydia's campaign highlights into a single scannable daily document

recent_evaluations.evidence_quote: PLACEHOLDER — no morning briefs evaluated yet
recent_evaluations.notes: Initial scorecard seeded. First evaluation triggers on first morning brief delivery (target 5:45am ET daily). Rubric: article-writer (shared Content team rubric). Key emphasis: synthesis quality (T4 outputs from T1 calendar + T1 research) and scanability (Marc reads in < 90 sec).

scorecards.biblical_alias_rationale: Ezra the Scribe (Ezra 7; Nehemiah 8) was the editor and teacher who synthesized the Law for the returning exiles — he read, explained, and applied the canonical texts so the people could act on them. CRG's Daily Brief Editor synthesizes raw research (Daniel), calendar data (Martha), and campaign signals (Lydia) into a morning document Marc can act on in < 90 seconds. Ezra's editorial role — not authoring the law, but making it comprehensible and actionable — is the exact function.
scorecards.note: Alias note: Ezra (Daily Brief Editor) is distinct from Asaph (SEO Manager). No naming collision — different roles, different biblical figures, different teams.

scorecards.name: Joseph
scorecards.role: Bookkeeper — transaction categorization, invoice tracking, Xero reconciliation across all CRG silos; supplies cost-structure actuals to Solomon for pricing decisions; flags financial exceptions to Marc within 24h

recent_evaluations.evidence_quote: PLACEHOLDER — no reconciliation cycles evaluated yet
recent_evaluations.notes: Initial scorecard seeded. First evaluation triggers on first completed weekly Xero reconciliation run. Rubric: bookkeeper (new, purpose-built for finance operations).

scorecards.biblical_alias_rationale: Joseph of Egypt (Genesis 39-47) was the archetype of administrative stewardship at scale — he managed Potiphar's house, then the prison, then Pharaoh's entire national supply chain and treasury. He categorized the surplus grain (7 years of recording and storing), tracked all incoming tribute, reconciled the granary records, and flagged the famine before it was a crisis. No biblical figure better represents meticulous, multi-ledger financial administration with an early-warning function. Joseph as Bookkeeper is the exact mapping.
scorecards.cra_compliance_note: All categorizations must be defensible under CRA business-expense rules. Personal expenses NEVER coded as business. Silo separation maintained for potential entity separation.

scorecards.biblical_alias_rationale: The Levites (Numbers 3:6-9; 1 Chronicles 9:26-27) were the priestly tribe assigned to guard the Tabernacle — specifically the gatekeepers who controlled access to the holy of holies. No unauthorized person entered; they kept the keys and the watch. CRG's Vault Keeper is the gatekeeper of the credential layer: nothing gets in or out without Levite's control, and no agent (including Claude) ever touches a raw API key.

scorecards.biblical_alias_rationale: Nehemiah rebuilt the walls of Jerusalem in 52 days (Neh 6:15) — the archetype of rapid, systematic infrastructure build under pressure. He coordinated workers, managed logistics, and shipped a complete defensible system on deadline. CRG's Automation Engineer is the infrastructure builder who wires the walls (webhooks, workflows, scripts) that protect and accelerate every other agent.

recent_evaluations.evidence_quote: PLACEHOLDER — no pricing recommendations evaluated yet

scorecards.biblical_alias_rationale: Solomon (1 Kings 3-4; 10:1-29) was the archetype of commercial wisdom — he managed trade routes, negotiated with Hiram of Tyre, set the tribute structure for 12 districts, and received the Queen of Sheba's revenue-intelligence visit. He is the canonical figure for pricing as applied wisdom: not just knowing market rates, but understanding the strategic value of positioning at every tier. Chosen over Joseph (logistics) and Daniel (research) specifically because Solomon's domain was commerce and valuation.
scorecards.escalation_threshold: any spend recommendation > $50 escalates to Marc
